chapter_title.en: The Prayer of the Two Eids

text: : «اَلْخُرُوجُ يَوْمَ اَلْفِطْرِ وَ اَلْأَضْحَى إِلَى اَلْجَبَّانَةِ حَسَنٌ لِمَنِ اِسْتَطَاعَ اَلْخُرُوجَ إِلَيْهَا» قَالَ فَقُلْتُ أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ كَانَ مَرِيضاً لاَ يَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ أَ يُصَلِّي فِي بَيْتِهِ فَقَالَ «لاَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1460 - Harun ibn Hamzah al-Ghanawi narrates from Imam Jafar Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Going out on the day of Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha to the open area (for prayer) is good for whoever is able to go."
Harun asked: "What if a person is sick and unable to go out, should he pray at home?"
Imam (as) replied: "No."
